fields.description:
## Context
Sprint operators who resume an interrupted sprint must currently remember to add --resume to their /dso:sprint command. Since the ticket system already tracks epic status as in_progress when a sprint has started, this flag is redundant — the orchestrator already has the information it needs to auto-detect a resume scenario. Forgetting --resume causes sprint to re-enter preplanning unnecessarily, wasting context and risking re-dispatch of already-completed work.

## Success Criteria
1. When /dso:sprint <epic-id> is invoked and the epic's status is in_progress, sprint automatically enters resume mode (prints a notice, runs drift detection scoped to open tasks only, then applies checkpoint resume logic) without requiring --resume
2. The --resume flag is removed from sprint's argument parser, usage documentation, and all cross-references (sprint SKILL.md, end-session SKILL.md)
3. Drift detection during auto-resume is scoped to open tasks only — in_progress and closed child tasks are excluded from the drift check to prevent false positives from the epic's own prior work
4. Sprint prints a brief notice (e.g., "Resuming in-progress epic — scanning checkpoints...") when auto-resume is triggered, so the operator knows resume mode is active
5. Dogfooding validation: the first real interrupted sprint after this change is resumed by running /dso:sprint <epic-id> (without --resume), confirming auto-detect triggers correctly, preplanning is not re-run, and drift detection completes without false positives from prior work

## Done Definitions
- When this epic is complete, running /dso:sprint <epic-id> on an in_progress epic automatically enters resume mode without requiring --resume
  <- Satisfies: SC1
- When this epic is complete, the --resume flag no longer appears in sprint SKILL.md, end-session SKILL.md, or any other documentation
  <- Satisfies: SC2
- When this epic is complete, drift detection during auto-resume only checks files referenced by open child tasks, excluding in_progress and closed tasks
  <- Satisfies: SC3
- When this epic is complete, auto-resume prints a visible notice confirming resume mode is active
  <- Satisfies: SC4
- When this epic is complete, dogfooding confirms auto-detect works on a real interrupted sprint without --resume
  <- Satisfies: SC5

## Scope
- IN: Auto-detect logic in sprint SKILL.md Validate Primary Ticket step, --resume flag removal from all docs, --status filter on sprint-drift-check.sh, zero-child guard (fall through to preplanning), resume notice output
- OUT: Changes to merge-to-main --resume (separate feature), changes to checkpoint format, changes to ticket status vocabulary

## Considerations
- [Testing] sprint-drift-check.sh needs test update for new --status filter argument
- [Maintainability] Auto-resume path must converge with existing checkpoint resume logic to avoid divergent behavior

## Dependencies
None

## Approach
At the "Validate Primary Ticket" step, check the epic's status. If in_progress, print a resume notice, run drift detection (scoped to open tasks via a new --status filter on sprint-drift-check.sh), then jump to the existing checkpoint resume logic. Remove the --resume flag from argument parsing and all documentation references. Guard: if auto-resume triggers but zero child tasks exist, fall through to preplanning gate.

## Scenario Analysis
- Epic marked in_progress from abandoned sprint triggers auto-resume on fresh start (high, runtime): If auto-resume triggers but zero child tasks exist (abandoned mid-preplanning), orchestrator must fall through to preplanning gate instead of Phase 3.
- Operators who scripted --resume in CI pipelines receive unknown-flag error (high, deployment): Covered by SC2 cross-reference cleanup.
- sprint-drift-check.sh does not accept a task-status filter argument (high, configuration): Script must be modified to accept --status filter for open-task scoping.

### Planning Intelligence Log
- Web research (Step 2.6): not triggered
  - Bright-line conditions that fired: none
- Scenario analysis (Step 2.75): triggered
  - Scenarios surviving blue team filter: 3
- Practitioner-requested additional cycles: none
